spec_match.rationale: The tour demonstrates await-based sequencing for programmatic smooth scroll promises, including a fallback when methods do not return thenables.

interactive.rationale: The page has start/reset controls, visible progress state, a timestamped event log, and a visible source panel that mirrors the tour loop.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The page still matches the parent guided 5-stop tour description.

edge_cases.rationale: The implementation keeps a non-promise fallback path and the conformance suite now checks attached scrollable elements, promise resolution, and scrollBy behavior.

references.rationale: The page now includes the canonical ChromeStatus link and the CSSOM View scrollTo spec link.

design_system.rationale: The added source panel uses shared CSS variables, explicit foreground/background pairs, and the existing bordered demo style.

openQuestions.title: Verify virtual tour in Chrome 150
openQuestions.detail: Use chrome-devtools-mcp to run the tour, observe the source panel remains visible, confirm each scroll promise settles before the next stop, and check the feature conformance page in the same browser.

summary: Added visible source code for the virtual tour and fixed the missing canonical references; browser verification is still pending because chrome-devtools-mcp is unavailable.
